_purpose: Declares every network destination this deployment may call. build/check-egress.mjs asserts no request succeeds to a destination outside allowed_egress_destinations.

allowed_egress_destinations.description: The founded EpiStack Competition Community, this deployment's default community: 57 claims (25 checked, the mechanical protocol claims plus the submission-decomposition's mechanical register plus the first admitted contribution; 21 constitutive, the submission-decomposition's own vocabulary-kernel definitions carried in by depends-on links and constitutive-register anchor spans alike; 11 asserted, the submission's own evaluative theses across both the founding claims and the decomposed documents, awaiting semantic acceptance), now adopting four shared kinds (measurement, forum, comment, declaration as of the Phase KG-11 submission-decomposition merge).

The founded Registry Community (Phase KG-12): 13 claims, 3 contract-bundle (constitutive: ranker contract, skin contract, client contract, each naming a required oracle by hash) and 10 artifact-card (checked: two shipped extensions, the assistant, two shipped skins, the minimal client, this app itself, vault and membrane as components, epistack-competition as a community entry), every artifact-card's conformance citation a real re-run against the actual shipped source at seeding time, no fabricated outcome.

The mirror (Phase KG-12 Step 5): this deployment's own governance kernel, joined as an ordinary browsable community, snapshot by hash like any other. 48 claims: the original 20 (all checked, grounding complete as of Phase KG-4), the 7-claim entrance listing, and the 21-claim front-page decomposition (Phase KG-front-page-claims), the same claims the front page's own claim lens (periphery/root-lens.js) reads from this identical snapshot. Its own repository (https://github.com/A-Viable-Fork/Knowledge-Game) is the contribution target for any claim about it.

The mirror (Phase KG-12 Step 5): upstream EpiStack's own math kernel, the one legacy corpus in the pinned submodule that already exports a kernel-native buildKernel(), emitted directly from that submodule and joined as an ordinary browsable community. Its own repository (https://github.com/A-Viable-Fork/epistack) is the contribution target for any claim about it; a promotion proposal read here is an ordinary claim, argued in the open, never a privileged act this app performs on the protocol's behalf.

The three rooms (Phase KG-13): the LHC room, the competition's clean case, emitted directly from upstream/epistack's own unmodified build/lhc-build.mjs at the re-pinned commit 6b3cbfcd5a63b126a5ac6740a1feb2f9e92d80f4. 25 claims, 28 links. Its own repository (https://github.com/A-Viable-Fork/epistack/tree/main/corpora/lhc) is the contribution target; the corpus lives there, never in this app.

The three rooms (Phase KG-13): the eggs room, the competition's corrupted-field case, at the re-pinned commit 6b3cbfcd5a63b126a5ac6740a1feb2f9e92d80f4. Merged from the three domain stores (nutrition, environment, economics) build/eggs-build.mjs builds over their one shared table (corpora/eggs/tables.js); the cross-domain composite weighing layer is not part of this snapshot, since local-provider.mjs's snapshot shape has no representation for a citation-graded composite claim (build/emit-rooms.mjs's own header documents this honestly). 61 claims, 23 links. Its own repository (https://github.com/A-Viable-Fork/epistack/tree/main/corpora/eggs) is the contribution target.

The three rooms (Phase KG-13): the covid room, the competition's adversarial case, emitted directly from upstream/epistack's own unmodified build/covid-build.mjs at the re-pinned commit 6b3cbfcd5a63b126a5ac6740a1feb2f9e92d80f4. 106 claims, 104 links. Its own repository (https://github.com/A-Viable-Fork/epistack/tree/main/corpora/covid) is the contribution target.

_allowed_document_destinations_purpose: Phase KG-11: the submission reading surface's document fetches, a distinct destination category from allowed_egress_destinations above (those go through api/community.js's fetchCommunity; these go through api/submission.js's loadDocument, verified against content_hash rather than snapshot_hash). build/check-submission-surface.mjs asserts no request succeeds to a destination outside this list.

allowed_document_destinations.description: The submission surface's first reading-sequence document, fetched from the pinned upstream commit and verified against its own anchor map's content_hash before rendering.
The submission surface's second reading-sequence document, fetched and verified the same way.
The submission surface's third reading-sequence document (the seam section), fetched and verified the same way.
